name: Muspelheim
shortDescription: Muspelheim is the primordial realm of fire in Norse cosmology, home to fire giants and demons. It is a place of intense heat and destruction, playing a crucial role in the creation and eventual destruction of the world.
description: Muspelheim, also known as Múspellsheimr, is one of the nine realms in Norse cosmology, representing the primordial source of fire and heat. It is located in the south, a realm of intense heat, flames, and volcanic activity. Muspelheim is inhabited by fire giants (also sometimes considered demons) and ruled by Surtr, a powerful fire giant who wields a flaming sword. According to Norse mythology, the sparks from Muspelheim mingled with the ice of Niflheim in the Ginnungagap, the primordial void, which led to the creation of the first being, Ymir. During Ragnarok, the end of the world, Surtr and the fire giants will march from Muspelheim to Asgard, setting the world ablaze and contributing to the final destruction. Surtr's flaming sword will engulf the earth, and the heat from Muspelheim will consume the cosmos. The realm's fiery nature is a stark contrast to the icy realm of Niflheim, and their interaction is a fundamental aspect of Norse creation myths and eschatology. Muspelheim represents chaos, destruction, and untamed elemental power, playing a vital role in the cyclical nature of the Norse universe.

location.cosmological: Located in the southern region of the Norse cosmos, a realm of extreme heat and fire.
